title: Long Jump · Progressions
subtitle: FUNetics 6-stage progression · Athletics 365
source: FUNetics 'Jumping for Distance: Long Jump' (card 18). Powered by Athletics 365.
tierNote: For primary-school grassroots coaching, Red → Yellow → Green covers the realistic span. Purple, Blue and Black stages are for athletics-club / competitive contexts where pupils have access to a sand pit and a marked run-up.

stages.name: Red Stage
stages.headline: Jump Variations
stages.summary: Exploration of jumping shapes and soft landing fundamentals.

progressions.text: Demonstrate various jumps in response to instructions (jumps for height, distance, 2 feet to 2 feet, etc.).
Demonstrate soft landing — bending at the knees to cushion impact.
Take off from, and land in, different positions.

stages.name: Yellow Stage
stages.headline: Standing Long Jump
stages.summary: Two-foot take-off, two-foot landing with arm swing for distance.

progressions.text: Use backward to forward arm swing to increase jumping distance.
Jump for distance from two feet to two feet.
Demonstrate control and balance on landing — feet shoulder width apart; centre of gravity over base.
Jump for distance from one foot to two feet (taking off from both feet).

stages.name: Green Stage
stages.headline: Short Approach Long Jump
stages.summary: Introduce a short run-up and preferred take-off leg into a sand pit.

progressions.text: Identify preferred take-off leg.
Use a short run (two strides) to jump from one foot to two feet (both feet).
Demonstrate soft controlled landing into a sand pit.
Focus is forwards, not downwards.
Move body parts in an effective order to aid jump distance and efficiency.

stages.name: Purple Stage
stages.headline: Long Jump · Approach + Take-off
stages.summary: Marked run-up of 10–12 strides with controlled active take-off.

progressions.text: Mark out a basic run up to achieve optimal speed and accuracy.
Demonstrate a fast controlled approach (optimum speed) with limited run up: 10–12 strides.
Demonstrate awareness of the take-off point.
Demonstrate 'Active' flat foot at take-off (dynamic down and back motion).
Demonstrate basic head up, chest up, drive forwards and up.

stages.name: Blue Stage
stages.headline: + Flight Phase
stages.summary: Refined approach speed, free-knee drive at take-off, controlled flight position.

progressions.text: Achieve optimum speed after a controlled run up.
Drive the free knee up and forwards.
Keep body upright and hips high at take-off.
Keep a tall upright body position in flight phase of jump.
Judge distances accurately.

stages.name: Black Stage
stages.headline: Full Long Jump
stages.summary: Consistent sprint approach, full extension at take-off, controlled landing.

progressions.text: Perform a consistent sprint technique on the approach run.
Take off with power and control after a fast run up.
Demonstrate full extension of hip, knee and ankle joint (in that order) at take-off.
Hold take-off extension (body tension) during early flight.
Land with heels first, and bend knees to absorb momentum.
